mcqs.question: What does Newton's First Law of Motion state?
options: A. A body at rest will stay at rest unless acted upon by an external force.
B. The acceleration of an object is directly proportional to the net force acting on it.
C. For every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction.
D. All objects fall at the same rate.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is an example of inertia?
options: A. A person sitting in a moving bus feels pushed back when the bus suddenly stops.
B. A ball rolling on the ground slows down due to friction.
C. A rocket launching into space.
D. A car accelerating.

mcqs.question: Inertia depends on which property of an object?
options: A. Mass
B. Volume
C. Shape
D. Color

mcqs.question: If a hockey puck slides on ice, it will continue to slide until:
options: A. It hits a wall.
B. A player hits it.
C. Friction slows it down.
D. It stops moving.

mcqs.question: An object will remain in motion unless:
options: A. It is stopped by friction.
B. It is acted upon by an unbalanced force.
C. It loses energy.
D. It reaches the end of its path.

fill_in_the_blanks.question: According to Newton's First Law, an object at rest will remain at rest unless acted upon by a __________.
The tendency of an object to resist changes in its state of motion is called __________.
An object's mass is a measure of its __________.
A __________ force is required to change the motion of an object.
Newton's First Law is also known as the law of __________.

short_questions.question: Give an example of Newton's First Law in everyday life.
What happens to a ball rolling on a surface if no external forces act on it?
Why is it important to wear seat belts in a car?
